nodes.title: Render an answer only while open
nodes.why: The child receives controlled state and uses built-in @if to keep closed answers out of the DOM.
nodes.alternative: Keep every answer mounted and toggle visibility with CSS alone.
nodes.tradeoff: Conditional rendering recreates answer DOM, but it keeps the visible and accessibility state aligned.

nodes.title: Track FAQ rows by stable question text

anchor.snippet: @for (item of faqItems; track item.question; let i = $index) {

nodes.why: The question is stable in this static collection and preserves child identity across parent updates.
nodes.alternative: Track by the current index regardless of item identity.
nodes.tradeoff: Question text must remain unique; a dedicated id would be better if content becomes editable.

nodes.title: Treat open indexes like a set
nodes.why: Multiple-open mode adds or removes only the selected index without disturbing other open items.
nodes.alternative: Replace the full open list on every toggle in both modes.
nodes.tradeoff: Two state branches add logic, but they preserve the distinct accordion modes.

nodes.title: Keep one index in single-open mode
nodes.why: Replacing the array with the selected index guarantees that only one answer stays open.
nodes.alternative: Append the index and clean up extra open rows later.
nodes.tradeoff: The mode closes any previously open answer immediately, which is the expected accordion behavior.
